meta.title: Nando — Andrej Kristancic, Goriška Brda
meta.description: Natural wines from 5.5 hectares of ponca soil, where Collio and Brda share a border and a terroir.

hero.text: I make wine on five and a half hectares that happen to sit in two countries. The border runs through my vineyards, but the soil doesn't know that.

place.text: My vineyards are in Plešivo, where the Italian Collio meets the Slovenian Brda. Sixty percent of the land is on the Italian side, forty on the Slovenian. The border is an unpaved road between the rows — I cross it several times a day without thinking about it. The soil is the same everywhere here: ponca, layers of marl and sandstone that crumble in your hands. The warm air comes up from the Adriatic, the cold drafts come down from the Julian Alps. In between, the grapes take their time.

method.text: I farm organically and let the fermentation start on its own — indigenous yeasts, nothing added. The whites macerate on their skins; how long depends on the variety and the year. I don't filter aggressively. What ends up in the bottle is what the ponca and the season gave me. There's not much I need to do if the grapes are right.

contact.title: If you're passing through Brda, come by. Call ahead.
